Items.Key: command injection via admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|6062|6068|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary shell commands with the privileges of the application user, potentially leading to full system compromise.|user executes a command like 'admin exec <command>' where <command> contains shell metacharacters.|sanitize the input command to remove shell metacharacters before passing it to system(), or use a whitelist of allowed commands. consider using popen() with a restricted shell or a dedicated command execution library.->command injection via admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|5606|5608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary shell commands with the privileges of the application user, potentially leading to full system compromise.|user executes a command like 'admin exec <command>' where <command> contains shell metacharacters.|sanitize the input command to remove shell metacharacters before passing it to system(), or use a whitelist of allowed commands. consider using popen() with a restricted shell or a dedicated command execution library.

command injection in audit event generation|os command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|5622|5632|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; system(command.c_str());|the 'audit_path' is derived from environment variables (temp or tmpdir). if an attacker can control these environment variables, they can inject shell commands into the audit log path, leading to command execution.|triggering the emergency override mechanism (5 failed attempts + 'emergency_override') which calls append_override_audit_event.|do not use shell operators (>>) in paths controlled by environment variables. use the c++ filesystem library to write to a known safe location like /tmp or a dedicated audit directory.

truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 successfully identified Command Injection in Config Validation (SC-V3-004), Hardcoded Emergency Override Bypass (SC-V3-007), and Insecure Temporary File Handling (SC-V3-013). It identified a Heap Overflow in the Expression Parser (SC-V3-009) but misclassified it as a generic Buffer Overflow and High severity. It missed the Stack Buffer Overflow (SC-V3-005) and Hardcoded Administrator Credentials (SC-V3-010).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file;
int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: The audited answer correctly identified the Command Injection vulnerability in the Config Validator function using the exact code pattern from the source.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A (Run 2 identified Heap Overflow in parser instead)
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified a Heap Overflow in the parser (SC-V3-009) but missed the Stack Buffer Overflow in the Input Processing function using `strcpy(input_buffer_, ...)`.
The audited answer correctly identified the Logic Bomb bypass mechanism in the authentication function.
The audited answer identified the code causing the overflow but mislabeled it as a generic 'Buffer Overflow' and assigned High severity instead of Critical severity.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A (Run 2 identified the bypass logic instead)
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the Logic Bomb bypass (SC-V3-007) but did not identify the Hardcoded Administrator Credentials constant (`ADMIN_SECRET` / `SC_ENT_2025_AUTH`).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:

Items.Notes: The audited answer correctly identified the Command Injection via Insecure Temporary File Handling.
